chapter_title.ar: حَدِيثُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ (عليه السلام) مَعَ يَزِيدَ
chapter_title.en: Hadith of Ali Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> with Yazeed<sup>la</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 229)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 229)
text: تَمَارَى النَّاسُ عِنْدَ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ (عليه السلام) فَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ حَرْبُ عَلِيٍّ شَرٌّ مِنْ حَرْبِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ حَرْبُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) شَرٌّ مِنْ حَرْبِ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) قَالَ فَسَمِعَهُمْ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ (عليه السلام) فَقَالَ مَا تَقُولُونَ فَقَالُوا أَصْلَحَكَ اللَّهُ تَمَارَيْنَا فِي حَرْبِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) وَ فِي حَرْبِ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) فَقَالَ بَعْضُنَا حَرْبُ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) شَرٌّ مِنْ حَرْبِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) وَ قَالَ بَعْضُنَا حَرْبُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) شَرٌّ مِنْ حَرْبِ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام)

فَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ (عليه السلام) لَا بَلْ حَرْبُ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) شَرٌّ مِنْ حَرْبِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَ حَرْبُ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) شَرٌّ مِنْ حَرْبِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) قَالَ نَعَمْ وَ سَأُخْبِرُكَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ إِنَّ حَرْبَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) لَمْ يُقِرُّوا بِالْإِسْلَامِ وَ إِنَّ حَرْبَ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) أَقَرُّوا بِالْإِسْلَامِ ثُمَّ جَحَدُوهُ.

en.hubeali: H 14801 – Yahya, from Abdulah Bin Muskaan, from Zureys who said:

The people disputed in the presence of Abu Ja’farasws. So some of them said, ‘The wars fought by Aliasws were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by the Rasool Allahsaww, and some of them said, ‘The wars fought by the Rasool Allahsaww were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by Aliasws’. He (the narrator) said, ‘So Abu Ja’far listened to them and said: ‘What are you all saying?’ So they said, ‘May Allahazwj keep youasws well, we are disputing with regards to the wars of the Rasool Allahsaww and the wars of Aliasws. Some of us are saying that the wars of Aliasws were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by the Rasool Allahsaww, whilst some of us are saying that the wars fought by the Messengersaww were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by Aliasws’.

So Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No! But, the wars of Ali<sup>asws</sup> were (against a people) more evil than the wars of the Rasool Allah<sup>saww</sup>’. So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>, the wars of Ali<sup>asws</sup> were (against a people) more evil than the wars of the Rasool Allah<sup>saww</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, and I<sup>asws</sup> shall inform you about that. The Messenger<sup>saww</sup> fought wars (against a people) who did not accept Al-Islam, and that the wars of Ali<sup>asws</sup> were (against a people) who accepted Al-Islam, then fought against him<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 354. Yahya, from Abdulah Bin Muskaan, from Zureys who said: The people disputed in the presence of Abu Ja’far (a.s.). So some of them said, ‘The wars fought by Ali (a.s.) were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by the Rasool Allah (saww), and some of them said, ‘The wars fought by the Rasool Allah (saww) were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by Ali (a.s.)’. He (the narrator) said, ‘So Abu Ja’far listened to them and said: ‘What are you all saying?’ So they said, ‘May Allah (azwj) keep you (a.s.) well, we are disputing with regards to the wars of the Rasool Allah (saww) and the wars of Ali (a.s.). Some of us are saying that the wars of Ali (a.s.) were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by the Rasool Allah (saww), whilst some of us are saying that the wars fought by the Messenger (saww) were (against a people) more evil than the wars fought by Ali (a.s.)’. So Abu Ja’far (a.s.) said: ‘No! But, the wars of Ali (a.s.) were (against a people) more evil than the wars of the Rasool Allah (saww)’. So I said to him (a.s.), ‘May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), the wars of Ali (a.s.) were (against a people) more evil than the wars of the Rasool Allah (saww)?’ He (a.s.) said: ‘Yes, and I (a.s.) shall inform you about that. The Messenger (saww) fought wars (against a people) who did not accept Al-Islam, and that the wars of Ali (a.s.) were (against a people) who accepted Al-Islam, then fought against him (a.s.)’.
